act_name_th: พระราชบัญญัติการประกอบธุรกิจของคนต่างด้าว

5.text_en: Section 5. The provisions of this Act shall not apply to the business operation of aliens under the law on investment promotion or under the law on industrial estate of Thailand in so far as it concerns the promoted activities or activities related to the establishment and operation of an industrial estate or activities in an industrial estate.

7.text_en: Section 7. A foreigner having a treaty to which Thailand is a party or has an obligation under which rights have been granted to operate business of a foreigner shall be governed by the provisions of such treaty.

15.text_en:
Section 15. A foreigner which is a juristic person may operate any business specified in List Two only where not less than forty percent of its shares are held by Thai nationals or juristic persons which are not foreigners under this Act, and the number of Thai directors shall not be less than two-fifths of the total number of directors.
The Minister, with the approval of the Cabinet, may reduce the proportion of shares specified in paragraph one, but such reduction shall not be less than twenty-five percent.

list_one.amendment_notes: List One businesses are absolutely prohibited to foreigners. No exceptions or permits available.

list_two.amendment_notes: Foreigners may operate List Two businesses only with permission from the Minister with Cabinet approval, and subject to the Thai shareholding requirements in Section 15.

list_three.amendment_notes: Foreigners may operate List Three businesses with permission from the Director-General with the approval of the Foreign Business Committee. List Three item (21) is a catch-all that captures most service businesses unless specifically exempted by Ministerial Regulation.
